title: Drypetes integerrima
taxonomy: Drypetes integerrima is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is a member of the genus Drypetes, which is native to tropical regions. The genus Drypetes is part of the order Malpighiales, within the class Magnoliopsida. This plant is also known as the Yellow Bells or the Golden Trumpet, due to its bright yellow flowers. The classification is based on its characteristics, such as its leaf arrangement and flower structure.
uses: Drypetes integerrima, a plant native to tropical regions, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes.
